journal.name: Georgetown Journal of Law & Public Policy

registry_refs.key: Georgetown Journal of Law & Public Policy
registry_refs.notes: Registry rank 122 currently duplicates the stale /in-print-2/ seed.
Existing sitemap documents article PDF paths but retains the stale seed.

publication_scope.notes: This is a WordPress subsite on a host shared with other Georgetown journals.

evidence.notes: No rule disallows /public-policy-journal/ or its WordPress upload paths.
Supplied and registry seed is stale; site navigation points to /public-policy-journal/in-print/.
Live page lists issue links from volume 23 issue 2 (Summer 2025) back through volume 18 number 2 (Summer 2020), including special issues.
Issue page title encodes volume, issue, season, and year; article groups appear under Articles and Notes headings, with article landing links in content and subnavigation.
Article page exposes title in h1.abstract_alt_volume_title, authors in div.published_author, a multi-paragraph full-text/abstract lead, and a direct WordPress PDF link.
Single bounded smoke returned PDF magic bytes and 392,959 bytes; server ignored the Range header and delivered the complete article PDF.

reconnaissance.notes: The live route is clear and file delivery works. Historical layout consistency remains untested because this wave intentionally stopped after the newest issue and one article.

include: Print issues and article pages under /public-policy-journal/in-print/
exclude: editor letters and front matter unless classified
scope.notes: Require the journal path and sites/23 upload directory; do not follow generic Georgetown links.

entry_points.notes: Replace the stale /in-print-2/ registry and sitemap seeds with this route.

nodes.notes: HTML article page with title, byline, body/abstract lead, and PDF link.

edges.notes: Anchor text carries volume, issue/special issue, season, and year.
Filter out breadcrumbs and deduplicate repeated subnavigation/content links.
Direct article PDF appears as the Continue reading link.

archive_patterns.notes: Slugs vary for number, issue, special issue, season, and year; parse labels rather than relying only on path tokens.
Article slug is nested beneath its issue.
Upload year can differ from issue year and must not be used as publication year.

pagination.termination: Single observed page lists visible issue navigation through volume 18 number 2.
pagination.notes: No next control observed; older volumes may be absent from the live navigation.

layout_eras.label: Current Georgetown journal WordPress layout
layout_eras.notes: Only the newest issue/article layout was sampled, so the era bounds are deliberately narrow.

sources.example: The Fiduciary Constitution, the Separation of Powers, and the Legal Landscape After SEC v. Jarkesy
sources.notes: Issue anchor text is a fallback.

title.missingness: Present on the sampled issue and article page.

sources.example: by Frank Garrison, John Kerkhoff and Elizabeth Slattery
sources.notes: Strip leading by and split conservatively.

authors.missingness: Present on the sampled article; issue page does not expose bylines.

sources.locator: main p before the Continue reading PDF link
sources.example: For decades, the American people’s right to a jury trial under the Seventh Amendment was severely curtailed...
sources.notes: The page contains extensive HTML full text; determine whether the opening paragraphs are an abstract or article body before labeling.

abstract.missingness: Substantial text is present on the sampled page, but abstract semantics are ambiguous.

sources.notes: Inherit issue year; never use the PDF upload year.

year.missingness: Present on the sampled issue heading and visible archive labels.

sources.notes: Season is not a precise date.

volume.missingness: Present on sampled issue and archive labels.

sources.notes: Special issues require a textual value.

issue.missingness: Numeric on sampled issue; archive also contains special issues.

pages.notes: PDF first-page extraction may recover it.

citation.missingness: No citation block observed in the bounded sample.

patterns.notes: Single smoke passed; server ignored Range and returned the full small PDF.

validation.notes: Response began %PDF-1.7; article-specific filename and landing-page context support article granularity.

robots.notes: No rule disallows the scoped journal or upload routes.

barriers.notes: The registry and existing sitemap seed /in-print-2/ now returns 404; /in-print/ is live.

access.politeness_notes: Robots was checked first. Requests were publication-scoped and only one small representative PDF was retrieved.

verdict.summary: The supplied /in-print-2/ route is stale, but the live /in-print/ archive exposes issue and article pages; the sampled article has title, authors, extensive text, and a direct PDF whose smoke passed.
blockers: Oldest and middle issue layouts were not sampled.
The live archive navigation visibly reaches only 2020, so earlier coverage needs a bounded discovery strategy.
Opening article text may be full text rather than a formal abstract.
verdict.next_action: Replace /in-print-2/ with /in-print/, sample one middle and one oldest visible issue, then run the mapped WordPress adapter with strict journal and sites/23 scope guards.
verdict.implementation_notes: Deduplicate issue/article links repeated in navigation and content; parse volume metadata from issue headings; use div.published_author for bylines; prefer sites/23 direct PDFs; never derive publication year from upload paths.
